gures by 16 and add
the quotient to the figure kept apart. Divide this result by 14.
To the quotient thus got add the constant Ahargana of 246.
This will give the number of days preceding the birth
i.e. the day of conception. Now let us apply this rule to
the same example ; By deducting the Lagna from the Moon
we get 162~-38'-49*. Keep this in two places and divide one
by 16. We get then 100-9'-55*. Adding this to the figure,
1625-38'-49', we get !72°-48'-44*. Dividing this by 14 we get
125-20'-90*. Adding the constant Ahargana of 246 to this
we get 256;'-20'-39*. This means that the conception took
place on the 256th day preceding the day of birth. Now to
find out the weekday, divide this number by 7. The remainder
is 4. The day of birth was a Saturday, So subtract 4 from 7,
number of Saturday which is the 7th day in the week. We
get 3 which indicates a Tuesday. This is the weekday
of Niseka. Still something more remains to be done : Divide
